steps.title: Capture Text two qualification
steps.content: Text 1 says the bees appeared to be playing because they were having fun. Text 2 does not fully reject that idea: it says bumblebees may engage in play and may experience some kind of positive emotional state.

steps.title: Keep the emotion limited
steps.content: Text 2 immediately qualifies the claim by noting that insects lack brain areas associated with human emotions and that insect emotions, if they exist, are likely very rudimentary. So the response should allow possible positive feeling but limit how complex that feeling is.

steps.title: Reject stronger replies
steps.content:
• Choice A wrongly changes the possible feeling from positive to negative stress.
  • Choice B claims complex emotional states in insects, which Text 2 does not support.
  • Choice C shifts to human play, a topic Text 2 never discusses.
The answer is D.
